ucs.title: [LLM] Ke3chang / NICKEL RoyalDNS implant — periodic DNS TXT-record C2 beaconing
ucs.description: Hunts for the RoyalDNS backdoor's signature C2 channel: a single endpoint repeatedly resolving TXT records on the same non-corporate FQDN, where the TXT body carries operator commands. NCC Group and Microsoft both attributed RoyalDNS exclusively to Ke3chang/APT15/NICKEL/Vixen Panda — TXT-only beaconing from an endpoint binary is essentially an APT15 fingerprint.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count dc(_time) as unique_seconds values(DNS.src) as src_hosts values(DNS.src_category) as src_cats min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen from datamodel=Network_Resolution where DNS.record_type="TXT" AND DNS.query!="" AND NOT (DNS.query IN ("*._dmarc.*","*._domainkey.*","*_mta-sts*","*_acme-challenge*","*_spf.*")) AND NOT DNS.query="*.local" by DNS.query DNS.src span=1m | stats sum(count) as total_q dc(_time) as active_minutes values(src_hosts) as src values(first_seen) as first values(last_seen) as last by DNS.query DNS.src | where total_q>=20 AND active_minutes>=10 | eval beacon_window_min=round((last-first)/60,1) | sort - total_q
ucs.kql:
let _legit_txt = dynamic(["_dmarc","_domainkey","_mta-sts","_acme-challenge","_spf","_amazonses","_atproto","_psl"]);
let _benign_resolvers = dynamic(["svchost.exe","msedge.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","OneDrive.exe","backgroundtaskhost.exe"]);
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(24h)
| where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
| extend Parsed = parse_json(AdditionalFields)
| extend QueryName = tostring(Parsed.QueryName), QueryType = tostring(Parsed.QueryType)
| where QueryType =~ "TXT"
| where isnotempty(QueryName)
| where not(QueryName has_any (_legit_txt))
| where not(QueryName endswith ".local" or QueryName endswith ".arpa")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_benign_resolvers)
| summarize Count = count(),
            ActiveMinutes = dcount(bin(Timestamp, 1m)),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            Procs     = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 5),
            Cmds      = make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 5)
            by DeviceName, DeviceId, QueryName
| where Count >= 20 and ActiveMinutes >= 10
| extend BeaconWindowMin = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| where BeaconWindowMin >= 10
| order by Count desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _legit_txt = dynamic(["_dmarc","_domainkey","_mta-sts","_acme-challenge","_spf","_amazonses","_atproto"]);
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where QueryTypeName =~ "TXT"
| where isnotempty(Name)
| where not(Name has_any (_legit_txt))
| where not(Name endswith ".local" or Name endswith ".arpa")
| summarize Count = count(),
            ActiveMinutes = dcount(bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            Resolvers = make_set(Computer, 10)
            by ClientIP, Name
| where Count >= 20 and ActiveMinutes >= 10
| extend BeaconWindowMin = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| where BeaconWindowMin >= 10
| order by Count desc
ucs.rationale: Ke3chang/NICKEL is one of the very few state actors that ships a DNS-TXT-only backdoor (RoyalDNS, observed in the UK government incident NCC documented). Generic egress-beacon detections won't catch it because the implant never opens a TCP socket — all C2 rides on TXT lookups your recursive resolver issues outward. Filtering out legitimate `_dmarc/_domainkey/_acme-challenge/_mta-sts` records and w

ucs.title: [LLM] Ke3chang / NICKEL diplomatic-target post-exploit recon command burst
ucs.description: Hunts for the tightly-clustered native Windows recon command set Ke3chang operators run by hand on diplomatic and MFA hosts: net group/view/user, ipconfig, systeminfo, netstat, tasklist, nltest, whoami, query — Mandiant's Operation Ke3chang report and Microsoft's NICKEL writeup both document this exact LotL fingerprint within minutes of initial access. The detection looks for ≥5 distinct recon binaries from one user-account on one host inside a 10-minute window.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process_name) as recon_run values(Processes.process) as cmdlines dc(Processes.process_name) as distinct_recon_bins values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("net.exe","net1.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","netstat.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","hostname.exe") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user _time span=10m | where distinct_recon_bins>=5 | eval window_sec=last_seen-first_seen | sort - last_seen
ucs.kql:
let _recon = dynamic(["net.exe","net1.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","netstat.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","hostname.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where FileName in~ (_recon)
| summarize DistinctReconBins = dcount(FileName),
            ReconRun = make_set(FileName),
            Cmds = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 25),
            Parents = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10),
            ParentCmds = make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 10),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen = max(Timestamp)
            by DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, 10m)
| where DistinctReconBins >= 5
| extend WindowSec = datetime_diff('second', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _recon = dynamic(["net.exe","net1.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","netstat.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","hostname.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend BaseName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where BaseName in (_recon)
| summarize DistinctReconBins = dcount(BaseName),
            ReconRun = make_set(BaseName),
            Cmds = make_set(CommandLine, 25),
            Parents = make_set(ParentProcessName, 10),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
            by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where DistinctReconBins >= 5
| extend WindowSec = datetime_diff('second', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Ke3chang/APT15 is one of the most heavily LotL-leaning Chinese clusters — Mandiant's Operation Ke3chang report literally lists `ipconfig /all`, `net group "Domain Admins" /domain`, `tasklist /v`, `netstat -ano`, `systeminfo` as the operator's first-touch recon batch on diplomatic / MFA hosts, and Microsoft's 2021 NICKEL writeup confirmed the same pattern persisted across BS2005, RoyalCli and Mirag
